name: active_directory
explanation: Active Directory inherits the Windows perf_counters template (same shape as iis). In its default mode the agent runs on the domain controller itself and reads NTDS / Netlogon / DHCP Server / DFS Replicated Folders performance counters locally; the check actively detects which Windows services exist on the host (see ActiveDirectoryCheckV2.get_default_config) and enables only the relevant counter sets. There is no remote host/port in the default deployment, so discovery reduces to 'is the NTDS service installed on this Windows host?'. The optional pdh_legacy mode adds remote host + credentials but is hidden in the spec.
notes: Pattern H (local Windows host detection), as anticipated. The check's _service_exists() probe is itself the discovery primitive.
